Enquiry.title: Enquiry
Enquiry.description: Represents a user's inquiry submitted through various forms on the website.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Enquiry entity. Auto-generated by Firestore.

name.description: Full name of the person making the inquiry.

phone.description: Contact phone number of the inquirer.

email.description: Optional email address of the inquirer.

class.description: The current class or academic status of the inquirer (e.g., 'Class 10', 'Dropper').

courseInterest.description: The specific course or program the inquirer is interested in.

message.description: Additional message or query from the inquirer.

source.description: The page or form from which the inquiry was submitted (e.g., 'home_form', 'contact_page').

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the inquiry was submitted, generated by the server.

ScholarshipLead.title: ScholarshipLead
ScholarshipLead.description: Represents an expression of interest for a scholarship program.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ScholarshipLead entity. Auto-generated by Firestore.

name.description: Full name of the student interested in the scholarship.

phone.description: Contact phone number of the student.

class.description: The current class or academic status of the student.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the scholarship lead was submitted, generated by the server.

CourseCategory.title: CourseCategory
CourseCategory.description: Defines a broad category of courses offered, used for homepage display and navigation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CourseCategory entity. This is the Firestore document ID and serves as the primary key.

title.description: The main title of the course category (e.g., 'JEE Main & Advanced').

subtitle.description: A short descriptive subtitle for the course category.

mode.description: The primary mode of classes for this category ('Hybrid', 'Offline', 'Online').

icon.description: A string identifier for the icon associated with this category.

order.description: Numerical order for displaying course categories.

active.description: Boolean indicating if the course category should be actively displayed on the website.

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the course category (e.g., 'jee'). This is the document ID and serves as a natural key.

SubCourse.title: SubCourse
SubCourse.description: Represents a specific, detailed course offering within a broader CourseCategory.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SubCourse entity. Auto-generated by Firestore.

categorySlug.description: Reference to the slug of the parent CourseCategory. (Relationship: CourseCategory 1:N SubCourse)

badge.description: An optional badge text for the sub-course (e.g., 'Most Popular', 'New Batch').

title.description: The main title of the sub-course (e.g., 'JEE 2 Year Program').

tags.description: Descriptive tags for the sub-course, where each item is a JSON string representing an object with 'icon' and 'label' properties.

description.description: A detailed description of the sub-course content and objectives.

highlights.description: A list of key features or benefits of the sub-course.

googleFormLink.description: An optional URL to an external Google Form for enrollment or inquiries specific to this sub-course.

enrollButtonEnabled.description: Boolean indicating if the enrollment button for this sub-course should be active.

enrollButtonLabel.description: The text displayed on the enrollment button.

order.description: Numerical order for displaying sub-courses within their category.

active.description: Boolean indicating if the sub-course should be actively displayed on the website.

definition.description: Stores high-level course categories for homepage display. Documents are identified by their 'slug' (which also serves as the document ID) and contain public, read-only data for category overview. Includes 'active' and 'order' fields for UI control.

params.name: categorySlug
params.description: The URL-friendly identifier for the course category, used as the document ID.

definition.description: Contains detailed sub-course offerings. Each document includes a 'categorySlug' field which acts as a denormalized link to its parent CourseCategory for efficient query filtering. All documents are public and read-only. Includes 'active' and 'order' fields for UI control, and 'id' as the document ID.

params.name: subCourseId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific sub-course, used as the document ID.

definition.description: Stores all user inquiries submitted via various forms on the website. Documents are created with 'serverTimestamp()' and are intended for public write access (via client forms), with read access implicitly restricted to administrators for lead management (not directly accessible via client-side read rules). 'id' serves as the document ID.

params.name: enquiryId
params.description: Unique identifier for a user enquiry, used as the document ID.

definition.description: Stores expressions of interest for scholarship programs. Documents are created with 'serverTimestamp()' and are intended for public write access (via client forms), with read access implicitly restricted to administrators for lead management. 'id' serves as the document ID.

params.name: leadId
params.description: Unique identifier for a scholarship lead, used as the document ID.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes clarity, performance for public read operations, and adherence to security principles by segregating data based on its access patterns. The `course_categories` and `sub_courses` collections are designed for public, read-only access, which is explicitly stated in the provided Firestore rules (`allow read: if true;`). This directly supports the QAPs (Queries Are Not Filters) mandate, as the rules grant full read access, and any filtering (e.g., by `categorySlug` or `active` status) occurs on the client side, simplifying rule logic.

Authorization Independence is achieved by explicitly denormalizing the linking information. The `SubCourse` documents, stored in the top-level `sub_courses` collection, include a `categorySlug` field. This allows the application to query for sub-courses related to a specific category (e.g., `/sub_courses?categorySlug=jee`) without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `CourseCategory` document within security rules. This design eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, making security rules simpler to write, test, and debug, while supporting atomic operations if write access were to be introduced for administrators.

Structural segregation is applied by placing public-facing, read-only content (`course_categories`, `sub_courses`) in separate collections from sensitive, write-only lead capture data (`enquiries`, `scholarship_leads`). The `enquiries` and `scholarship_leads` collections are intended for public write (create) access via forms, with read access implicitly restricted to administrators, ensuring sensitive data is not publicly exposed. This segregation ensures that each collection has a homogeneous security posture, making the overall security model robust and easy to reason about.
